description_long: This is a traditional wooden outrigger ship with a single large tan sail mounted on a central mast. The hull is elongated and narrow, with two prominent dark-colored outriggers extending from either side, providing stability in the water. The deck is open and simple, with visible rigging lines connecting the mast to the hull and outriggers. The bow and stern are both slightly upturned, and the wood grain texture is visible along the sides. The sail is rectangular and slightly curved, held taut by ropes. There are no visible cabins, superstructures, or mechanical equipment on the deck.
description: A wooden outrigger ship with a large tan sail, open deck, and two dark outriggers for stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the upturned bow, central mast with tan sail, and symmetrical outriggers extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated hull, the full height of the mast and sail, and the length of the outriggers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the stern, the back of the sail, and the symmetrical placement of the outriggers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the hull profile, mast, and outrigger structure.
attribute_hull_design: The hull is a narrow, elongated displacement type with a pointed bow and stern, both slightly upturned. Two large, flat outriggers are attached on either side, providing lateral stability. The hull appears to be constructed from wood planks, with no visible keels or bulbous bows.
attribute_superstructure_layout: There is no superstructure; the deck is open with a single central mast and visible rigging. No cabins, bridges, or enclosed spaces are present.
